mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate collapsing household demand that forces corporate earnings downgrades and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects expanding factory output and rising corporate revenues that generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate profit margins, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS drops below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or forward returns are not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate below 1.5 percent combined with falling housing starts signals severe demand contraction that triggers earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a hit rate above 53 percent across all contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing 110.0 indicates high capacity utilization and strong manufacturing demand that drives immediate positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where INDPRO exceeds 110.0.
